title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After Archibald departs, Inspector Sharp interrogates Nigel Chapman regarding the death of Celia Austin. Nigel, a twenty-five-year-old student of Bronze Age and Medieval History at the University of London, was born in Nagasaki. He claims to have no romantic interest in Celia, despite seeing her daily, and notes that she disliked his sense of humor. Nigel recounts that the group last saw Celia at dinner, where Colin McNaughton announced their engagement. While the group later moved to a public lounge, Nigel claims he did not personally serve Celia coffee and denies any involvement in her death or the theft of items from the house.

When questioned about green ink found on Mr. Johnston's paper, Nigel suggests someone used his ink to frame him due to "resentment" among the residents, though he becomes evasive when pressed for details. Inspector Sharp also considers the possibility that Celia’s thefts were a psychological issue or a ploy to gain Colin's attention.

The Inspector then questions Leonard Bateson, who admits to pouring coffee from a pot into a cup and placing it next to Celia, but denies adding morphine. Bateson claims he was distracted by an argument and did not see her drink it. Bateson becomes angry, insisting Celia must have poisoned herself. Sharp counters this by revealing that the suicide note was actually a piece of a letter Celia had written earlier that morning, suggesting the note was a forgery.
